caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing rules and guidelines for Inherited IRAs and Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs). The page explains that RMDs may be required for IRAs, retirement plan accounts, or Roth IRAs inherited from the original owner. It provides links to "Retirement Topics - Beneficiary" for information on taking RMDs from inherited accounts and reporting taxable distributions as part of gross income. Additionally, it mentions Publication 559, which assists executors and administrators in completing federal income tax returns and paying taxes on behalf of the deceased. The video also briefly touches on the 2020 coronavirus-related distribution, noting that RMDs were waived for that year. The cursor is visible, indicating that the user is navigating through the page, possibly highlighting or clicking on relevant sections.
